title: marsdenia tirunelvelica
intro: Marsdenia tirunelvelica, also known as the Tirunelveli milk vine, is a perennial plant native to the Tirunelveli district of Tamil Nadu, India. It is a species of milk vine, a flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, known for its milky latex and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Marsdenia tirunelvelica is often prepared as a decoction or powder, and is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat conditions such as bronchitis, cough, and cold.
